title: Build a Hermes Knowledge Base That Self-Improves
summary: The video demonstrates how to augment AI agents like Hermes with self-improving knowledge bases by combining retrieval-augmented generation principles with automated ingestion pipelines. It connects personal knowledge management tools (Obsidian wiki), LLM services (Claude, NotebookLM), and meeting capture (Granola) via cron jobs to create a two-way memory system that persists knowledge across agent interactions.
writeup: Jack Roberts demonstrates building a self-improving knowledge base for AI agents like Hermes by pairing retrieval-augmented generation with automated ingestion pipelines. The build connects an Obsidian wiki, LLM services (Claude and NotebookLM), and meeting capture through Granola, wiring them together with cron jobs to form a two-way memory system. The result persists knowledge across agent interactions rather than losing context between sessions. Key takeaways: automated ingestion keeps the knowledge base current, and the two-way design lets agents both read from and write back to persistent memory.
